text: The problem is all pair shortest path in this video we will look at a problem and we will solve it using dynamic programming approach Then i'll show you what is the formula how we get the formula for dynamic programming And also I will show you a piece of code for solving the problem So let us start what the problem is the problem is to find a shortest path between every pair of vertices Let us say the starting vertex is 1 then I should find out a path From 1 to 2 1 to 3 and 1 to 4 Then again selecting 2 as a starting vertex. I should find a shortest path to vertex 1 and 3 and 4 Similarly from 3 I should find a shortest path from 1 2 and 4 from 4 to 1 and 2 and 3 So this looks similar to single source shortest path that is Dijkstra algorithm But Dijkstra algorithm will find out a shortest path from one of the source vertex And Dijkstra takes order of n square time If I run Dijkstra algorithm On all the vertices one by one that is all n vertices then I can get the result. Yes I can use the extra algorithm for solving this problem for all n vertices one by one And for each vertex it will take n square time and the time will be order of n cube So this problem can be solved using greedy method also But let us now see how we can apply dynamic programming for solving this problem Now how the problem can be solved using dynamic programming dynamic programming says that the problem should be solved by taking sequence of decision In each stage, we will take a decision So what decision I have to take let us see see if I have to find a shortest path between vertex 1 to 2 So there may be a direct edge path from 1 to 2 also like here you can see Or maybe there is a shorter path going via vertex 3 Or it may be going via vertex 4 So in this way I have to check or decide whether a shorter path is going via some other vertex So i'll start Selecting the middle vertex as vertex 1 So i'll find out first whether there is a shorter path between all the pair of vertices going via vertex 1 Then via vertex 2 and so on. So this is how we can take decision or sequence of decisions So how this can be done? This can be done by just preparing matrices explaining this adjacency matrix see here from 1 to 1 or 2 to 2 4 to 4 there is no edge.
